Inspect or modify authors in the project

Usage:
  laurel authors [--config <path>] [--orphaned] [--json] [--dry-run] <subcommand...>

Arguments:
  subcommand          `list` (show authors and post counts) or `rename <old-slug> <new-slug>`

End of options:
  Use `--` before positional values that start with `-`, for example `laurel new post -- --config`.

Options:
  -c, --config <path> Config path(s); repeat or comma-separate to deep-merge in order
  --orphaned          Show only authors that are defined under content/authors/ but referenced by zero posts (`list` only)
  -j, --json          Emit results as JSON for CI consumption (`list` and `rename`)
  --dry-run           On `rename`: scan and report the files that would change without writing anything
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > .laurelrc > config > default).
  Naming: LAUREL_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --config → LAUREL_AUTHORS_CONFIG

Repeated flags:
  Scalar string flags use the last value.
  List-style string flags accumulate as comma-separated values in argv order.
  Boolean flags use the last positive or negated spelling.

Examples:
  laurel authors list                          # all authors + post counts
  laurel authors list --orphaned               # authors defined but unused by posts
  laurel authors list --json                   # machine-readable author inventory
  laurel authors rename old-author new-author
  laurel authors rename old new --dry-run       # preview files that would change
